summary_line: New Jersey passive-real-estate-syndication coaching and investment-club led by Managing Partner Alina Trigub — woman-owned, explicitly niched at 'IT Leaders' for the wealth-building pitch. Multi-surface brand: SAMO Investing Club membership · coaching · public speaking · free 'Portfolio Management 101' report · newsletter · blog · video lessons · library. Same structural category as Peoples Capital Group (NJ-based passive real-estate syndication) but at smaller scale and with a coaching/community surface added on top of the deal flow.
regulatory_note: SAMO Financial is a passive-real-estate-syndication coaching and investment-club business led by Managing Partner Alina Trigub — not an SEC/state-registered investment adviser. Site explicitly describes the offering as 'investing club' membership + coaching + access to syndication deals, which keeps the firm in the educational-publisher + Reg D-syndication carve-out rather than the RIA-registration perimeter. The pipeline's enrich-adv correctly returned no IAPD match. Structurally similar to Peoples Capital Group on the syndication side and to Karlton Dennis / Dealmakers on the personal-brand-coaching side. Worth a manual FINRA / NJ Bureau of Securities lookup on next refresh if the firm becomes more material; Alina Trigub may be individually registered or licensed.

notable_events.event: Site captured: hero 'Simplifying Passive Real Estate Investing for IT Leaders' — explicit niche-targeting at IT-industry professionals as the ICP. Managing Partner: Alina Trigub (named on About page; alina@samofinancial.com; (201) 281-7430). Stated as a 'woman-owned business' that 'empowers women to take charge of generating wealth.' Product set: SAMO Club (membership), Coaching, Public Speaking, Newsletter, free 'Portfolio Management 101' special report, Video Lessons, Library, Blog. Conversion funnel: Join the Club → Let's Talk → Take Action (invest in syndications) → Reap the Rewards. Three named testimonials with first-name + last-initial attribution (Derrick K, Eugene S, plus a reference to a WWWIRE — Women Who Want to Invest In Real Estate — meetup talk in New Jersey). Charity component noted but specific causes not surfaced in captured copy. Trigub previously appeared as keynote speaker at NJ Women's real-estate investing community events. Same NJ geographic concentration as Peoples Capital Group; both occupy the 'passive RE syndication for mid-career professionals' adjacency to our wealth-management category.
notable_events.source: tools/extract.ts (5 pages — home / about / services / blog / contact)

_schema.competitor_type: wealth_mgmt_traditional | wealth_mgmt_content_led | wealth_mgmt_solo | wealth_mgmt_tax_specialty | robo_advisor | bank_or_wirehouse | education_platform | b2b_network | finance_coach | tax_or_cpa_firm
_schema.threat_level: high | medium-high | medium | low — see _context/taxonomy.md for the four-axis rubric
_schema.last_updated: ISO date of most recent refresh
_schema.next_review_by: ISO date — when this profile should be refreshed (default last_updated + review_cadence_days)
_schema.review_cadence_days: default 90 — shorten for high-threat competitors (e.g. 30), lengthen for watch-only (e.g. 180)
_schema.team_size: solo | small | mid | large — categorical bucket; the actual integer goes in employee_count
_schema.advisor_count: integer count of registered investment advisor representatives (from Form ADV Part 1A Item 5.B)
_schema.employee_count: integer total employees (from Form ADV Part 1A Item 5.A)
_schema.branch_count: integer count of offices/branches (from Form ADV Part 1A Section 1.F)
_schema.aum_usd: most-recently-reported regulatory AUM in USD, from Form ADV Part 1A Item 5.F
_schema.aum_as_of: ISO date of the ADV filing the AUM was reported in
_schema.pricing_model: transparent_tiers | aum_only_disclosed | opaque | subscription | mixed_with_commissions | membership_fee | gated_via_funnel
_schema.fee_schedule_published: true if a fee table is visible on the public site without gating
_schema.fee_aum_pct_low|high: AUM fee as decimal, e.g. 0.0075 for 0.75% — range across tiers; null if not disclosed
_schema.fee_flat_low|high_usd: annual flat planning fee range in USD; null if not disclosed
_schema.min_account_usd: stated account minimum in USD, null if none or not disclosed
_schema.segment_early_adults: strong | ok | weak | not_aimed — fit against the Early Adults segment from _context/our_thesis.md
_schema.segment_mid_career_401k: strong | ok | weak | not_aimed — fit against the 40-50 401(k) segment
_schema.regulatory_jurisdiction: US | CA | UK | AU | EU | multiple | none (controlled vocab — see _context/taxonomy.md)
_schema.regulator: Free-form string. Common values: SEC, state (NAME), CIRO + FSRA + AMF, FCA, ASIC, none, multiple. The actual regulator(s) the firm answers to. Use 'none' for content platforms and other non-registered entities.
_schema.regulatory_note: Optional sentence explaining what's missing vs. an ADV-style dataset because of jurisdiction (vs. missing because we haven't pulled it). Null for US SEC-registered firms where we got the standard ADV pull.
_schema.ownership_structure: founder_led | solo_owner | employee_owned | pe_backed | public | private_partnership | network_collective | private_content_business
_schema.disciplinary_disclosures: true if any DRPs are on file at IAPD or in ADV Part 2A Item 9
_schema.disciplinary_disclosure_count: integer count of disclosure reporting pages
_schema.design_tone: establishment | modern | energetic | minimalist | editorial | b2b_professional
_schema.positioning_claims: controlled tag list — see _context/taxonomy.md for canonical vocabulary
_schema.vulnerabilities: controlled tag list — see _context/taxonomy.md for canonical vocabulary
_schema.tags: controlled tag list for cross-cutting attributes (e.g. fee_only, pe_backed, niche_specialist) — see _context/taxonomy.md
_schema.related_competitors: array of slugs in Competitors/ that this competitor is meaningfully compared with
_schema.trajectory.aum_history: [{as_of: ISO date, value_usd: int, source: 'ADV' | 'press' | 'site'}] — chronological, oldest first; populated from historical ADV filings
_schema.trajectory.notable_events: [{date: ISO, event: string, source: string}] — acquisitions, leadership changes, new disclosures, product launches, ownership changes
_schema._applicability: Fields prefixed sec_/crd_/aum_/fee_/disciplinary_/advisor_count apply to SEC-registered RIAs. For non-RIA entities (b2b_network, education_platform, finance_coach) leave null and rely on tags + positioning_claims instead. See _context/taxonomy.md § Entity-type applicability.
